info.name: NCA Toolkit API
info.description: Smoke + integration tests for the NCA toolkit API. See docs/postman/README.md.

exec: pm.test('Returns a job_id we can poll', () => {
console.log('Check webhook receiver at', pm.environment.get('webhookUrl'), 'in a few seconds');

item.name: 11 — Legacy v0 routes
item.description: Pre-v1 endpoints kept for backwards compatibility. Mostly thin wrappers; prefer the v1 equivalents in new code. Each entry documents the v1 successor in its description.

request.description: Mix an external audio track onto a video. Defaults to keeping both at 100% and using the video's length for output. No v1 equivalent yet — closest pattern is /v1/ffmpeg/compose with amix filter.

Duck the original video audio to 30%, keep external audio at 100%, end output when the audio track ends (output_length='audio'). 'id' is echoed back in the response envelope — useful as a caller-side correlation key.
Legacy auth-check endpoint. Same behavior as /v1/toolkit/authenticate; prefer the v1 path in new integrations.
Burn-in captions from an inline SRT string. The schema requires exactly one of {srt, ass}. v1 equivalent: /v1/video/caption with the same `srt` field but richer style options.

body.raw:
{
  "video_url": "{{sampleVideoUrl}}",
  "ass": "[Script Info]
ScriptType: v4.00+
PlayResX: 1920
PlayResY: 1080

[V4+ Styles]
Format: Name, Fontname, Fontsize, PrimaryColour, OutlineColour, BackColour, Bold, BorderStyle, Outline, Alignment
Style: Default,Arial,48,&H00FFFFFF,&H00000000,&H80000000,1,1,2,2

[Events]
Format: Layer, Start, End, Style, Text
Dialogue: 0,0:00:00.50,0:00:02.50,Default,Hello from ASS
Dialogue: 0,0:00:02.50,0:00:05.00,Default,Line two
",
  "options": [
    { "option": "FontName", "value": "Arial" },
    { "option": "PrimaryColour", "value": "&H00FFFFFF" }
  ],
  "id": "postman-legacy-caption-ass"
}

request.description: Burn-in pre-built ASS subtitles. The `options` array overlays libass style overrides. Useful when callers have generated ASS upstream (e.g. with /v1/media/generate/ass).
Concatenate two videos end-to-end. v1 equivalent: /v1/video/concatenate (same payload shape).
Extract every I-frame from the video as a JPG, upload to cloud storage, return the URL list. CPU + I/O heavy on long videos — prefer the webhook variant below for anything > 1 min.
Same operation queued. Returns 202 immediately with a job_id; final URL list arrives at webhook_url. Watch {{webhookUrl}} for the POST.

exec: pm.test('Status is 200 (or 400 if folder_id placeholder)', () => pm.expect([200, 400, 500]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Upload a file to Google Drive. Requires `file_url`, `filename`, `folder_id`. The runtime SA must have Editor on the target folder — share the folder with the SA email (see [SESSION-NOTES.md](../../SESSION-NOTES.md)). 400/500 here usually means the folder_id placeholder hasn't been replaced or the SA lacks Drive access.
Full payload: explicit `mime_type` (skips detection), 5 MiB chunked upload (`chunk_size`), async via `webhook_url`. Use this shape for large files.
Static image → 4-second 30 fps video. Range: length 1–60s, frame_rate 15–60. v1 equivalent: /v1/image/convert/video.
Ken Burns-style zoom. `zoom_speed` is 0–100 — higher = more aggressive zoom over the clip length.
Strip audio track to mp3 at default bitrate. v1 equivalent: /v1/media/convert/mp3.
Compressed mp3 at 64 kbps mono. Useful for keeping payloads under OpenAI's 25 MB transcription cap (~52 min at 64k). Bitrate must match pattern `^[0-9]+k$`.
Plain text transcript. v1 equivalent: /v1/media/transcribe (richer output, includes word-level timing).
SRT subtitle file. Feed the result directly to /v1/video/caption or /caption-video.
ASS subtitle file with each Dialogue line capped at 40 chars (good for mobile-vertical layouts). `max_chars` reflows long segments.

item.name: 12 — Additional v1 routes
item.description: v1 endpoints not covered in folders 1–10: dedicated ASS generator (richer than caption-video's auto-ASS) and S3-compatible uploader.

request.description: Run Whisper + emit an ASS file with default style (classic, white-on-black, Arial 24, bottom-centered).
TikTok-style vertical (1080×1920) ASS with highlight mode: the current word is colored yellow (&H0000FFFF), rest white, 3 words per line, all-caps. Burn-in with /v1/video/caption afterwards.
Clean up filler words via `replace`, drop the intro 1.5s via `exclude_time_ranges`. Both edits happen post-Whisper, pre-ASS-write — the original audio is untouched.

Pipe a remote file into the configured S3 bucket and mark it world-readable. Requires S3_* env vars set on the service — the GCS-only deploy will 500 here. Mirror of /v1/gcp/upload for S3-compatible providers (DO Spaces, R2, Backblaze).

Private upload (no ACL change). `download_headers` are sent on the SERVER side when fetching `file_url` — useful when the source requires a Referer or a signed-cookie/auth header. The headers never leak to the recipient.
Same upload queued. 202 + job_id back immediately, final URL POSTed to webhook_url. Recommended for files > a few hundred MB to avoid Cloud Run request timeouts.
